revisions.issue: main.ts does not currently call initScene() or startLoop() — the plan assumes they are already wired. Step 4 should explicitly add those calls (importing from src/visual/scene.ts and src/visual/renderLoop.ts) rather than just stating they 'must be called independently'. This is necessary to verify AC 'Visuals continue even if autoplay is blocked'.

Graph order stated as source → AnalyserNode → GainNode → destination is correct for muted analysis, but the design note claiming 'element.muted would disconnect the signal from our graph' is inaccurate. MediaElementSource still feeds data with element.muted=true in most browsers; the real reason to prefer GainNode muting is consistency and explicit control. Clarify the rationale to avoid misleading future readers.

Generating a valid silent mp3 programmatically is non-trivial. Prefer committing a real minimal silent mp3 file (e.g. 1-second silence, ~2KB) rather than describing it as 'generated silent buffer'. The file must be a valid mp3 or HTMLAudioElement.play() will reject with a decode error, breaking tests in real browsers.

Track switching via 'ended' event with createMediaElementSource has a subtle constraint: you cannot call createMediaElementSource on an element that is already connected. The plan should specify reusing the same HTMLAudioElement (changing its src) rather than creating new elements, since a single element can only have one MediaElementSource.

summary: Plan is well-structured and covers all acceptance criteria. The audio graph design (source → analyser → gain → destination) correctly preserves analysis data while muted. Two medium issues: (1) main.ts wiring in step 4 needs to explicitly add initScene/startLoop calls since they don't exist yet, and (2) track switching must reuse the same HTMLAudioElement rather than creating new ones due to createMediaElementSource constraints. Low-severity notes on the muting rationale and placeholder audio approach. No blocking issues.
